title: Array Index
subtitle: An element's position
goal: Learn to find array elements by their index.
explanation: Every element in an array has its own number.
This number is called an index.
The first element always has index 0.
You can use an index to access any element in an array.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine shelves in a cabinet. Each shelf has a number, so you can easily find the item you need.

remember: An index is an element's number.
Elements are accessed using square brackets [].

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners often think the first element has index 1. In JavaScript, indexing starts at 0.

quiz.question: What is the index of the first element in an array?
